title: JetSpec: Breaking the Scaling Ceiling of Speculative Decoding with Parallel Tree Drafting
abstract: arXiv:2606.18394v3 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: Speculative decoding (SD) accelerates autoregressive Large Language Models (LLMs) by drafting multiple tokens and verifying them in parallel, but it faces a scaling limitation: increasing the draft budget improves speed only when acceptance remains high and drafting overhead stays low. This ceiling has been difficult to break because prior head-based SD methods face a causality-efficiency dilemma. Autoregressive drafters produce path-conditioned candidates that are effective for tree speculative decoding with higher acceptance length, but their drafting cost grows with tree depth. Bidirectional block-diffusion drafters generate all positions in one pass, but their branch-agnostic marginals can form individually plausible yet mutually inconsistent trees, wasting budget and reducing acceptance. We propose JetSpec, a head-based SD framework that combines one-forward drafting efficiency with branch-wise causal conditioning. JetSpec train

Title: JetSpec: Breaking the Scaling Ceiling of Speculative Decoding with Parallel Tree Drafting
Abstract: arXiv:2606.18394v3 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: Speculative decoding (SD) accelerates autoregressive Large Language Models (LLMs) by drafting multiple tokens and verifying them in parallel, but it faces a scaling limitation: increasing the draft budget improves speed only when acceptance remains high and drafting overhead stays low. This ceiling has been difficult to break because prior head-based SD methods face a causality-efficiency dilemma. Autoregressive drafters produce path-conditioned candidates that are effective for tree speculative decoding with higher acceptance length, but their drafting cost grows with tree depth. Bidirectional block-diffusion drafters generate all positions in one pass, but their branch-agnostic marginals can form individually plausible yet mutually inconsistent trees, wasting budget and reducing acceptance. We propose JetSpec, a head-based SD framework that combines one-forward drafting efficiency with branch-wise causal conditioning. JetSpec train
translated_title: JetSpec：用并行树草稿打破推测解码的扩展上限
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.18394v3 公告类型：替换 摘要：推测解码（SD）通过草拟多个令牌并并行验证它们来加速自回归大型语言模型（LLM），但它面临一个扩展限制：只有在接受率保持较高且草拟开销保持较低时，增加草拟预算才能提高速度。这一上限一直难以突破，因为之前基于头的SD方法面临着因果-效率困境。自回归草拟器生成路径条件候选，这些候选对于具有更高接受长度的树推测解码非常有效，但它们的草拟成本随着树深度的增加而增长。双向块扩散草拟器在一次传递中生成所有位置，但它们与分支无关的边缘分布可能形成个体上合理但相互不一致的树，浪费预算并降低接受率。我们提出JetSpec，这是一个基于头的SD框架，它将一次前向草拟效率与分支级因果条件相结合。JetSpec训练
tldr: JetSpec通过结合一次前向草拟效率和分支级因果条件，解决了推测解码中因因果-效率困境导致的扩展上限问题。
en_tdlr: JetSpec breaks the scaling ceiling of speculative decoding by combining one-forward drafting efficiency with branch-wise causal conditioning, addressing the causality-efficiency dilemma.
